title: Sort all mounts by destination directory
body:
Currently depending on the sort order of mount points, we can overmount
a volume specified from the user. Podman has a function sortMount that
sorts all mounts based on destination directory to ensure all mounts
show up. This PR moves the function from Podman to Buildah. Once merged
I will change Podman to use the buildah function.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

fwiw I was able to pull the util.go and run_linux.go changes from here into an openshift/builder development image, run it against an OCP user provisioned install on top of rhel worker nodes, and verify that host entitlements work

we have a work around in openshift/builder for this, but ideally we'd like to revert that and pull in this change into openshift/builder's vendor tree through normal go mod vendor processing.

thanks

whether we can wait for this getting into something openshift/builder can vendor in vs. move forward with our workaround (which results in some noisy warning messages when we run on RHCOS) most likely will depend on whether any of growing number of customer use cases around host entitlements for OCP builds results in some escalations or not
@gabemontero we need another LGTM before it can merge.  @rhatdan @nalind @vrothberg all happy green test buttons, can you slap on a LGTM and get this merged please?
I see @gabemontero comments as a LGTM
